title: try_set_nickname: read-then-write race between HasNickname check and UPDATE
description: store::try_set_nickname races on concurrent same-user requests: SELECT sees nickname=NULL, then UPDATE WHERE nickname IS NULL gets rows_affected=0 when another connection wins the write first. The code returns Ok(SetNicknameResult::Set) regardless of rows_affected, so the losing device gets Set instead of HasNickname. In practice this requires two simultaneous device connections for the same pub_id both calling SET_NICKNAME — uncommon but not impossible. The second device's response is wrong (Set instead of HasNickname) and triggers a spurious USER_NICKNAME broadcast. Fix: check rows_affected from the UPDATE result and return HasNickname when rows_affected=0 and no unique violation occurred.
